text.title: Task Structure and Token Budget
text.summary: INVEST criteria, token budget tiers, task definition fields, and acceptance criteria rules for implementation plan tasks.

rule.statement: Every task must pass INVEST: Independent (can start after its declared deps complete), Negotiable (implementation flexible), Valuable (traces to FR-X), Estimable (token estimate declared), Small (under 100k tokens), Testable (acceptance criteria are verifiable).
rule.rationale: Tasks that fail INVEST produce incomplete agent executions. The most common failures are: not Independent (hidden deps cause ordering conflicts), not Small (context exhaustion mid-task), not Testable (no way to validate completion).

antipattern.description: Task with acceptance criteria that say "works correctly" or "functions as expected".
antipattern.reason: Vague AC cannot be verified by a test or a quality gate check. Every AC must specify a concrete observable outcome: a status code, a file that exists, a database row, a rendered element.

rule.statement: Assign each task to a tier: Simple (under 30k, single file, clear pattern), Normal (30-60k, multiple files, some exploration), Complex (60-100k, many files, novel solution). Tasks over 100k must be split before execution begins.
rule.rationale: Agents degrade non-uniformly near context limits. The 100k rule provides a safe margin for most model families. Declaring a tier forces estimation before execution.

example.code:
### TASK-003: Create user authentication middleware
- **Complexity:** Normal - **Tokens:** ~45k - **AD-X:** AD-002 - **FR-X:** FR-005, FR-006 - **Depends on:** TASK-001 (user model), TASK-002 (JWT utility) - **Blocks:** TASK-005 (protected routes)
**Files:** - CREATE `src/middleware/auth.py` - MODIFY `src/app.py` — register middleware
**Acceptance Criteria:** 1. Given valid JWT, when request arrives, then `request.user` is populated. 2. Given expired JWT, when request arrives, then 401 response is returned. 3. Given missing Authorization header, then 401 is returned.

list.item: Title: action verb + target (e.g., "Create auth middleware")
AD-X and FR-X trace references (min 1 each)
Blocks (what tasks need this to complete)
Files with action (CREATE / MODIFY / DELETE) and purpose
